input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue with keyboard handling in .NET 10. This is a known change — in .NET 9+, `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed because Android itself deprecated `adjustResize`. The recommended approach changed.

Let me provide a concise answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI's evolution:

In .NET 10 (and starting with .NET 9 updates), the recommended approach is to use the `KeyboardAcceleratorsChanged` or more specifically, Android's `WindowInsetsCompat` API via the `Edge-to-Edge` changes. But for MAUI specifically, the solution involves using `Android:Window.SoftInputMode` in the Android platform configuration, or using the newer API.


The real issue is that `adjustResize` was deprecated in Android API 30+, so the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach no longer works reliably. For .NET MAUI in .NET 10, I should recommend using `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` which handles this automatically, or properly managing window insets for edge-to-edge layouts instead of relying on the deprecated soft input mode adjustment. For chat screens specifically, wrapping content in a `ScrollView` with a `Grid` layout—input pinned at the bottom—tends to work well. On Android, I might need to explicitly set the soft input mode in `MainActivity.cs` to ensure `AdjustResize` behavior is applied correctly, since the default Android handling changed in API 30. The real issue is that `adjustResize` behavior changed with Android's edge-to-edge enforcement in newer versions, especially Android 15+, which affects how the keyboard interacts with layouts. For .NET 10 targeting these newer Android versions, I need to handle `WindowInsets` properly rather than relying on the deprecated `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize`. The most practical solution is leveraging `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` which is enabled by default, but if that doesn't work well for a chat screen layout, I should manually manage insets or restructure the layout to work with the new edge-to-edge behavior.
